title: Bridge Essay: Neural and phenotypic representation under the free-energy principle.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.

**Neural and phenotypic representation under the free-energy principle.**. Neurosci Biobehav Rev.. PMID: 33271162. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2020.11.024. The aim of this paper is to leverage the free-energy principle and its corollary process theory, active inference, to develop ageneric, generalizable model of the representational capacities of living creatures; that is, atheory of phenotypic representation. Given their ubiquity, we are concerned with distributed forms of representation (e.g., population codes), whereby patterns of ensemble activity in living tissue come to represent the causes of sensory input or data.
